headline: Kenya and France strengthen their strategic partnership
description: Nairobi will host the 'Africa Forward' summit on May 11 and 12, co-organized with Paris. In recent years, the two countries have steadily reinforced their cooperation.

China's green industries profit from Middle East war
description: The surge in fuel prices sparked by tensions around the Strait of Hormuz is accelerating global demand for Chinese energy transition technologies, further strengthening Beijing's industrial lead in electric vehicles and solar panels.

headline: In Madagascar, Russia seeks a new African stronghold
description: After bursting onto the scene alongside the junta, which seized power in October 2025, by providing it with weapons and military instructors from Africa Corps, Moscow has been advancing its pawns on many fronts.

headline: Spanish woman priest challenges the Vatican
description: Christina Moreira advocates for women's right to the priesthood, and was excommunicated by the Vatican for seeking the role. She continues her vocation as a parish priest in Galicia, disturbing figures high up in the Catholic Church.

headline: Who is Iyad Ag Ghali, the jihadist behind the destabilization of Mali?
description: A central figure in Sahelian jihad for nearly two decades, pursued in turn by the French and the Russians, Ghali led the offensive against the junta in Bamako. Since 2012, the jihadist and leader of Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) has softened his rhetoric, seeking to appear more approachable and less radical.

description: Aware of the potentially devastating impact their technology could have on jobs and society, the leaders of OpenAI and Anthropic are advancing a range of progressive ideas aimed at preserving social cohesion.

headline: In Hungary, jubilation marks Péter Magyar's inauguration as prime minister
description: After securing a decisive victory in April, the pro-European conservative pledged to 'work to ensure the rule of law returns quickly' and, above all, not to repeat the mistakes of his nationalist predecessor, Viktor Orban.

headline: Military deterrence has fully entered 'third nuclear age'
description: India, Pakistan, Ukraine, North Korea, Iran: The military revolution has also impacted the nuclear weapon debate, notably with the end of transparency and new announcements of increases to global arsenals, including in France.
